diagnostics.interpretation: Rupture aux jonctions de détecteurs, calibration locale ou sonde différente.
diagnostics.label: Splice / raccord détecteurs

diagnostics.interpretation: Décalage systématique entre campagnes, instruments ou référence blanche.
diagnostics.label: Erreur calibration / référence blanche

diagnostics.interpretation: Artefacts numériques ou traitement spectral incorrect.
diagnostics.label: Erreur interpolation / rééchantillonnage

diagnostics.interpretation: Effet systématique du support, blanc/noir, transflectance ou environnement de mesure.
diagnostics.label: Fond différent

diagnostics.interpretation: Combinaison possible changement de sonde + splice, amplifiée par géométrie, fond ou calibration.
diagnostics.label: Signature VERA25-like

diagnostics.interpretation: Modification de l'illumination, collecte, angle ou distance sonde-échantillon.
diagnostics.label: Différence de sonde / géométrie

diagnostics.interpretation: Couverture partielle du spot; contribution du fond ou du support.
diagnostics.label: Mélange feuille + fond

top_classes.name: Blue Mountain, Methuen Twp., Ontario, via Ward's Scientific (Cat.No. W-17)
from Kilauea Iki Drill core, via Rosalind Helz, USGS, Reston, VA(Cat. No. KI 79-3-100)
from Escondido, San Diego County, California, via Ward'sScientific (Cat. No. W-28)
Sample No. 381, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.
Sample No. 387, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.
Sample No. 384, The Hunt and Salisbury Collection at the U.S.Geological Survey, Denver, Co.

A medium-grained leucocratic rock showing some foliation, consisting of plagioclase, nepheline and biotite. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.intermediate.solid.syenit2.spectrum.txt

A gray, medium-grained, slightly vesicular rock withplagioclase laths less than or equal to 3 mm, opaques and a green phase approx. 1-2 mm in size, set in a grayish groundmass. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.solid.basal5.spectrum.txt
A dark gray medium-grained rock composed of plagioclase, a mafic mineral and minor opaques. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.igneous.mafic.solid.gabbro1.spectrum.txt

This is a very clean fine-grained to microcrystalline carbonate with little, if any, trace of clay. There is, however, a trace of opaque material present.Particle size was 500 - 1500 Micrometers.s Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.limestone.coarse.limest3.spectrum.txt

A well-sorted sample, consisting of medium fine-grained subangular to subrounded quartz grains set in an abundant matrix of yellowish-brown clay (kaolinite and/or illite?). The quartz grains contain numerous fluid inclusions, and there are trace amounts of plagioclase, tourmaline (schorlite), microcline, and opaques.Particle size was 500 - 1500 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.shale.coarse.shale1c.spectrum.txt

Slightly porous sample of whitish to brown oolites of phosphatic material, ~0.5 - 1.5 mm in size, collophane (amorphous calcium carbonophosphate, yellowish brown and isotropic) in oolitic form, frequently zoned. Fine-grained angular to subangular quartz occurs throughout, often in ooid cores. Silica recrystallization in some spots, carbonaceous material in oolite cores and rims are present. Particle size was 500 - 1500 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.shale.coarse.shale2.spectrum.txt

This is a very fine-grained, lineated/laminated sample consisting of brownish clay and very fine quartz particles (about equal amounts), along with tiny slivers of another phase (feldspar?). There are abundant small flecks and discontinuous lenses of carbonaceous and opaque material present as well.Particle size was 500 - 1500 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.siltstone.coarse.siltst1.spectrum.txt

This sample contains a microcrystalline groundmass or matrix of quartz, carbonate, and possibly some clay. There are irregular regions of larger anhedra of carbonate and quartz, with some grains of muscovite, glauconite, and green biotite.Particle size was 500 - 1500 Micrometers. Original ASTER Spectral Library name was jhu.becknic.rock.sedimentary.siltstone.coarse.siltst2.spectrum.txt
Meerdink et al. 2019, Baldridge et al. 2009
